chapter_title.en: Chapter 106 – Selling the juice and the wine

text: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ نَصْرَانِيٍّ أَسْلَمَ وَ عِنْدَهُ خَمْرٌ وَ خَنَازِيرُ وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ هَلْ يَبِيعُ خَمْرَهُ وَ خَنَازِيرَهُ وَ يَقْضِي دَيْنَهُ قَالَ لَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from one of our companions,

(It has been narrated) from Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a Christian who became a Muslim and with him was wine and pigs, and upon him were debts. Can he sell his wine and his pigs and fulfil his debts?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No’.
